chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 407 of 2018 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 07.02.2019 Mr. Munir Ahmed, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Mst. Ashraf Amin has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Revised Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 17/A, which were left by her deceased husband, namely, Muhammad Shabir Chughtai s/o. Qamer-ud- din, who allegedly died on 08.10.2006 at Karachi, leaving behind Mst. Ashrat Amin (widow/petitioner), Faiza Chunghtai (daughter), Muhammad Fawad Chughtai (son), Muhammad Faraz Chughtai (son) and Muhammad Farjad Chughtai as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable properties are also filed at page 15, 17 and 27 to 49. Legal heirs No. 2 to 5 have filed their affidavits of no objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Muhammad Usman s/o. Muhammad Ibrahim and Muhammad Saeed Akhtar Khan s/o. Muhammad Lateef Khan have also filed their respective affidavits 2 and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 12.12.2018; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 17.01.2019.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 12.12.2018; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 17.01.2019. On 31st January, 2019 petitioner as well as legal heirs No. 2 to 4 and above named witnesses made their appearance, while legal heir No. 5 has appeared today. All have affirmed the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verified their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable properties/assets shown in the Revised Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
